LAW AND MOTION 301, DEFENDANT CITY & COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. PLATTE'S SEPTEMBER 2005 AND NOVEMBER 2005 OFFERS TO CONTINUE PROCESSING DEBRIS ON-SITE DID NOT SATISFY PRINICIPAL'S CONTRACTUAL OBLIGATION OR BOND OPTION TO TIMELY REMOVE PRINCIPAL'S PERSONAL PROPERTY OR TERMINATION OF LEASE. THEREFORE, REJECTION OF THOSE OFFERS DID NOT EXONERATE PLATTE'S OBLIGATION TO PAY PENAL SUM, WHICH IT HAS NOT DONE. IT IS UNDISPUTED THAT TRUCKING DEBRIS FROM SITE WILL COST MORE THAN $650,000.00. THEREFORE, PORT IS ENTITLED TO JUDGMENT IN ITS FAVOR FOR $650,000.00. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR #8189.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Dec-15-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Kristina Poplawski, Esq., Deputy City Attorney, for defendant and moving party CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO
James DiWik, Esq. of Sedgwick, Detert, Moran & Arnold, 415-781-7900 for plaintiff and opposing party PLATTE RIVER INSURANCE COMPANY

RULING:
ARGUED and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows --
DEFENDANT CITY & AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. PLATTE'S SEPTEMBER 2005 AND NOVEMBER 2005 OFFERS TO CONTINUE PROCESSING DEBRIS ON-SITE DID NOT SATISFY PRINICIPAL'S CONTRACTUAL OBLIGATION OR BOND OPTION TO TIMELY REMOVE PRINCIPAL'S PERSONAL PROPERTY OR TERMINATION OF LEASE. THEREFORE, REJECTION OF THOSE OFFERS DID NOT EXONERATE PLATTE'S OBLIGATION TO PAY PENAL SUM, WHICH IT HAS NOT DONE. IT IS UNDISPUTED THAT TRUCKING DEBRIS FROM SITE WILL COST MORE THAN $650,000.00. THEREFORE, PORT IS ENTITLED TO JUDGMENT IN ITS FAVOR FOR $650,000.00. Prevailing party to submit form of order.
Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR #8189. (301/PJB/JF)
